title: Long Island Sound Restoration and Stewardship Reauthorization Act of 2025
short_title: Long Island Sound Restoration and Stewardship Reauthorization Act of 2025
stage_label: Referred to the Subcommittee on Water Resources and Environment.
plain_summary: This bill allows the government to keep paying for programs that help clean up and protect Long Island Sound for five more years, from 2025 to 2029. Long Island Sound is a large body of water between New York and Connecticut that is home to many fish and other sea animals. The Environmental Protection Agency runs these programs to make sure the water stays healthy and to fix any damage that has already happened. The bill also fixes a small mistake in how the laws are numbered.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Water Resources and Environment.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Long Island Sound Restoration and Stewardship Reauthorization Act of 2025.

sections.title: Section 2 Reauthorization of Long Island Sound Programs
sections.text: This section updates three existing laws that provide money for Long Island Sound programs. First, it changes the dates in the Federal Water Pollution Control Act so that Long Island Sound grants will be available from 2025 through 2029 instead of 2019 through 2023. Second, it makes the same date change in the Long Island Sound Stewardship Act of 2006, so stewardship grants will also be available from 2025 through 2029 instead of 2019 through 2023. Third, it makes a small fix to the numbering in the Federal Water Pollution Control Act by changing paragraph 4 to paragraph 3.
